title: Springtime
description: Spring is a collection of photographs made across a single March-to-May season, tracing the slow, unannounced arrival of warmth in ordinary places — a coat left unhung by a front door, the first meal eaten outside, mud on a doorstep, windows thrown open in rooms that have been sealed for months. The work is less interested in blossom and spectacle than in the in-between moments: the days that can't quite decide what season they belong to, the quality of light that exists only for a few weeks before it tips into something else entirely. Shot on medium format film, the images have a softness that feels earned rather than applied — pale greens, watery skies, the particular blue of an afternoon that's warmer than it has any right to be.
